data.title: Artificial Intelligence ETF AIQ comes into focus as the White House makes a robotics push
data.description:
The White House on Wednesday signaled a renewed push to accelerate its robotics and artificial intelligence agenda as the U.S. works to keep pace with China’s rapid technological advancements.

The announcement has drawn fresh attention to AI- and robotics-focused exchange-traded funds, with the Global X Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF (AIQ) emerging as a notable standout. 

AIQ, which holds 86 positions and manages roughly $7B in assets, is designed to provide exposure to companies poised to benefit from the continued development and deployment of AI technologies. Its portfolio spans firms integrating AI into products and services, as well as those supplying the hardware needed to power large-scale data analysis and other AI-driven applications.

Below, Seeking Alpha broke down the fund's top 10 holdings and ranked the list by their SA Quant ratings: 

No. 1: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Quant Rating of 4.97.

No. 2: AMD (AMD), Quant Rating of 4.79.

No. 3: Alphabet (GOOGL), Quant Rating of 3.49.

No. 4: Broadcom (AVGO), Quant Rating of 3.49.

No. 5: Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY), Quant Rating of 3.48.

No. 6: Apple (AAPL), Quant Rating of 3.47.

No. 7: Cisco Systems (CSCO), Quant Rating of 3.47.

No. 8: Tesla (TSLA), Quant Rating of 3.36.

No. 9: Alibaba (BABA), Quant Rating of 3.34. 

No. 10: Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), No Quant Rating provided. 

For a more in-depth look at the robotics and AI ecosystem, the following section highlights a range of exchange-traded funds offering diversified access to the space: 

Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (ARKQ), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).

data.title: U.S. stocks rise as investors shrug off lower-than-expected jobs data, struggling tech trade
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Wednesday as the Nasdaq Composite ((COMP:IND)) inched up to recover after the latest private sector jobs data and Microsoft's decline dragged sentiment.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.3% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.8%.

Microsoft (MSFT) shares fell after opening as The Information reported that the tech giant is cutting software sales quotas tied to artificial intelligence. The company -- currently -2% -- later refuted the report. 

The U.S. private sector employment fell by 32K in November, compared with the 5K consensus and October's 47K (revised from 42K), according to data released by ADP on Wednesday.

“It’s a K-shaped economy and labor market as the Nov ADP private payroll estimate indicated a 32K decline in hiring. For the past 6 out of 7 months small businesses have seen losses and were down -120K. These are firms without adequate capital depth and are likely experiencing stress in adjusting to higher prices and taxes such as tariffs,” said Joseph Brusuelas, RSM U.S. principal and chief economist.

“However, mid-size businesses added 51K and large firms added 39K as those businesses have exposure to the upper spur of the K that is doing quite well right now,” Brusuelas added.

Also, the S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI came in softer than expected at 54.2 in November, compared to October's 54.6, while the U.S. ISM Services Index edged up in November, beating consensus.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) continued its recovery, now over $93,000.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.07%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 3.50%.

On the earnings side, Salesforce (CRM) and Snowflake (SNOW), among others, will report their results.

In other economic news, the U.S. import and export prices were unchanged month-over-month in September.

data.title: Market Voices: Bessent on tariff options; Pentagon's F/A-XX program
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks. 

  • Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the Trump administration would be able to reinstate its trade strategy under the 1962 Trade Act if the Supreme Court rules it overstepped its bounds by implementing tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.

“We can recreate the exact tariff structure with [sections] 301, with 232, with 122,” Bessent said during an interview with CNBC's Andrew Ross Sorkin at the New York Times DealBook Summit, according to CNBC.

CNBC noted that Section 122 allows for tariffs to be imposed for 150 days, while Sections 301 and 232 are more vague in terms of timeframe. 

  • Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth reiterated that he is against the Navy's plans to build a next-generation F/A-XX fighter jet over concerns that its development will overlap with that of a next-generation aircraft for the Air Force, the F-47, that's been greenlighted by President Trump.

In a letter to lawmakers last month, Hegseth said that the Pentagon "strongly supports its original fiscal 2026 request reevaluating the F/A-XX program due to industrial base concerns of two sixth-generation programs occurring simultaneously," according to Bloomberg.

Northrop Grumman (NOC) and Boeing (BA) have been competing for the F/A-XX contract. The new aircraft would replace the Boeing F/A-18 Super Hornet, Bloomberg said.

Bloomberg noted that the U.S. Senate Armed Services Committee has been supportive of the F/A-XX program, adding $500 million in research funding for the program in this year's defense bill.

  • Meanwhile, U.S. Sen. Mitch McConnell reiterated his support for the F/A-XX program in an opinion piece published by The Wall Street Journal on Wednesday that argued for increased investment in the nation's military.

"The president announced his selection of a sixth-generation fighter aircraft for the Air Force, the F-47, but this program is underfunded in 2026 by $500 million. Pentagon dithering over the Navy’s sixth-generation fighter, the F/A-XX, has delayed its development and led to hundreds of millions in contract-extension costs," McConnell wrote in his op-ed.

"If the department made a decision, Mr. Trump could launch a program that ensures the aircraft carrier remains America’s premier power-projection platform for decades," McConnell added.

data.title: Regeneron downgraded at Morgan Stanley on valuation level
data.description:
Morgan Stanley has downgraded Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) to equal-weight from overweight saying that the stock is fairly valued.

The bank maintained its price target of $767 (~3% upside based on Dec. 2 close).

Analyst Terence Flynn noted that Eylea HD (aflibercept) recently gaining an additional indication for macular edema following retinal vein occlusion, as well as US FDA approval of a monthly dosing option for all indications, along with strong Dupixent (dupilumab) sales, have helped shares rebound.

"In our view from a high level perspective much of the focus for the stock will remain centered on the company's ability to execute on its diversification strategy away from Eylea (biosimilar competition on the market) and Dupixent (LOE March 2031 (US)/2033 (EU) per 10-K)," he wrote.

Flynn added he would like to see more clarity on Regeneron's pipeline diversification.

The company has several upcoming catalysts. Flynn highlighted phase 3 melanoma and phase 2 non-small cell lung cancer data of datafianlimab plus Libtayo (cemiplimab) and a decision on further itepekimab development in COPD, which it is working on with Sanofi (SNY). Regeneron is also examining the asset in nasal polyps and bronchiectasis.

In 2026, Flynn projects revenues of $14.89B (consensus $14.92B) and EPS of $45.19 (consensus $42.99).

data.title: Maersk says demand from India increasing as hopes rise for U.S.-India trade deal
data.description:
A.P. Moller-Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) is experiencing increased demand from India as expectations grow that a tariff deal with the U.S. will be reached, The Wall Street Journal reported Wednesday.

The shipping giant said in a customer advisory that its MECL service - which connects India with the Middle East, North Africa and North America - is operating with consistent weekly departures and that demand is expected to rise further if a U.S.-India trade deal is reached.

ING analyst Deepali Bhargava said India's crude oil imports from Russia have declined significantly, while imports from the U.S. have surged, signaling a deliberate diversification of suppliers.

"We see a high probability of an India-U.S. trade deal emerging in 2026, which could strengthen the rupee and support foreign institutional investor inflows amid strong growth and supply chain resilience," Bhargava said in a note.

Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) also said in the customer advisory that it is monitoring events around the Red Sea and Suez Canal following the Gaza ceasefire, although it still has no specific timing to restart sailing through the Red Sea.

data.title: Reasons to be bullish on small-caps heading into 2026 – BofA
data.description:
Bank of America strategists are bullish on small cap stocks (IWM), (SP600) in 2026, expecting them to outperform both mid- (SP400) and large caps (SP500) after facing numerous challenges in 2025.

“The long-awaited profits rebound is here,” said Jill Carey Hall, U.S. equity strategist and head of U.S. Small/Mid-cap Strategy, in a note. She cited consensus expectations of 18% EPS growth for small caps compared to 16% for mid-caps and 13% for large caps in the coming year.

Earnings growth will be the primary driver of small cap outperformance, with historical data showing small caps have outperformed by 9 percentage points on average in environments where their profit growth outpaces large caps and is accelerating.

 

 

 

“Small caps outperformed large caps 75% of the time in these backdrops over the last ~25 years,” Hall noted.

Federal Reserve rate cuts will also provide another tailwind, as BofA economists expect three more rate reductions between now and the end of next year. These cuts will help mitigate refinancing risks for small caps, which have nearly half their debt in short-term or floating-rate instruments.

“Each further 25bp cut reduces the cumulative hit by another ~2ppt,” Hall explained, highlighting the sensitivity of small cap earnings to interest rate movements.

The expected U.S. capital expenditure cycle should also particularly benefit small caps, whose sales growth has historically been highly correlated with U.S. capex trends. This relationship, combined with potential relief from tariffs and regulatory burdens, creates a favorable environment for smaller companies.

“Small caps have been the most at risk from tariffs given their thinner margins,” Hall said, noting that any reduction would disproportionately benefit these companies.

Current market positioning also supports the bullish outlook, as multi-cap managers are significantly underweight small caps relative to mid and mega caps. The bank’s research shows small caps make up just 1.8% of these portfolios, with managers approximately 60% underweight compared to market representation, even as small cap stocks have “finally begun to see single stock buying by clients this year after selling most of the last eight years.”

data.title: Hewlett Packard Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is set to post its results for the fourth-quarter on Thursday, after market close.

Wall Street expects the Texas-based firm to post EPS of $0.58 on revenue of $9.91 billion, implying a rise of over 16% during the quarter.

The company is seeing strong demand in its server and networking segments, fueled by the buildout of AI infrastructure from big tech companies and startups. Earlier in September, Hewlett Packard Enterprise delivered a third quarter beat, even though its initial fourth quarter guidance came in slightly soft.

Hewlett Packard Enterprise at a meeting with analysts in October, also issued weak guidance for its fiscal 2026. The stock has declined around 10% over the past month.

Piper Sandler said it appreciates the company’s fresh opportunities, the AI-systems growth, aspects of the Juniper deal and Greenlake transition. However, analyst James Fish added that “past execution, consistently changing reporting structure, and lack of margin uplift have weighed on investor sentiment.”

J.P. Morgan suggested that Nvidia's third-quarter results and guidance demonstrated positive momentum for AI infrastructure and is a good news for server solutions companies such as Dell and HP Enterprise.

Investors will closely monitor operating margin during the quarter, given the rapid AI expansion. HPE completed the $14 billion acquisition of Juniper in early July but the deal is facing renewed scrutiny.

HPE is strategically expanding its AI infrastructure by teaming with industry leaders such as AMD and Nvidia. Recently, HPE has also secured a $931 million cloud contract with U.S. Defense Department.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, HPE is rated Hold, while Wall Street analysts suggest buying the stock.

Seeking Alpha analyst Michael Del Monte said the company is positioned for growth after the Juniper acquisition, noting that “with more unified data center and hybrid cloud offerings, HPE is positioned to gain substantial traction in the global data center market.”

Over the last two years, HPE has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 75% of the time.

In the last three months, EPS estimates have seen eight upward revisions and three downward revisions. Revenue estimates have seen six upward revisions versus five downward moves.

The stock has risen over 3% year to date, compared with the over 16% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

data.title: Micron to exit Crucial consumer business
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) is shutting down its Crucial consumer business as part of its ongoing portfolio transformation to align its business to "secular, profitable growth vectors in memory and storage."

“The AI-driven growth in the data center has led to a surge in demand for memory and storage. Micron has made the difficult decision to exit the Crucial consumer business in order to improve supply and support for our larger, strategic customers in faster-growing segments,” said Sumit Sadana, EVP and Chief Business Officer at Micron Technology.

Micron (MU) will continue Crucial consumer product shipments through the consumer channel until the end of fiscal Q2 (February 2026). It will work closely with partners and customers through this transition and will provide continued warranty service and support for Crucial products.

The company intends to reduce the impact on team members due to this business decision through redeployment opportunities into existing open positions within the company.

MU is --2.55961% to $233.36

Source: Press Release

data.title: Top SA Quant-rated AI and robotics stocks ranked according to momentum indicator
data.description:
Robotics (ROBO)(ARKQ)(BOTZ) is in the spotlight on Wednesday, after a report that the Trump administration is looking into intensifying its focus on it as part of its broader AI initiative.     

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick is reportedly actively engaging with industry leaders and considering an executive order to boost the sector’s growth, primarily to compete with China’s dominant position in industrial robotics deployment.

Seeking Alpha compiled top Quant-rated AI and robotics stocks, finding nine names, and then took a look at their Relative Strength Index ((RSI)). 

RSI is a momentum indicator that measures the speed and magnitude of a security’s recent price changes to evaluate overvalued or undervalued conditions. A reading above 70 suggests overbought conditions, while a reading below 30 indicates oversold.

According to Seeking Alpha's analysis, AMD (AMD) is the most overbought stock on the list, with an RSI reading of 78.19. Six of the nine stocks have readings of 30 or below. Here is the full list:

  1. AMD (AMD) - RSI: 78.19; Seeking Alpha Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.79).
  2. Information Services Group (III) - RSI: 64.37; Seeking Alpha Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.55).
  3. Fanuc (OTCPK:FANUY) - RSI: 32.29; Seeking Alpha Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.66).
  4. BigBear.ai (BBAI) - RSI: 30.11; Seeking Alpha Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.72).
  5. Analog Devices (ADI) - RSI: 28.48; Seeking Alpha Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.51).
  6. Tuya (TUYA) - RSI: 26.26; Seeking Alpha Quant Rating: Buy (3.72).
  7. UiPath (PATH) - RSI: 12.51; Seeking Alpha Quant Rating: Buy (4.43).
  8. Radware (RDWR) - RSI: 2.89; Seeking Alpha Quant Rating: Buy (3.88).
  9. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology (ZK) - RSI: -5.95%; Seeking Alpha Quant Rating: Buy (4.36).

data.title: Crypto M&A hits record $8.6B as Coinbase, others drive big deals - report
data.description:
Deal values in the crypto space have surged to a record $8.6B this year through Nov. 20, driven by major acquisitions from industry heavyweights like Coinbase Global (COIN) and Kraken (KRAKEN), Bloomberg reported, citing data from PitchBook.

“Major crypto companies have become more acquisitive in 2025, with rate cuts, regulatory clarity and the crypto bull market earlier in the year shifting them into growth mode,” said PitchBook analyst Ben Riccio, Bloomberg reported. 

Record crypto M&A this year comes -- blowing past the previous $4.6B record in 2021 -- on the heels of Coinbase's (COIN) $2.9B takeover of Deribit, Kraken's (KRAKEN) acquisition of NinjaTrader, and Ripple's $1.25B deal to buy prime broker Hidden Road. On Tuesday, Kraken said it agreed to buy tokenized stocks platform Backed Finance.

Coinbase (COIN) was involved in 24 deals in the past five years, and eight deals in the past 12 months, PitchBook data showed, Bloomberg reported. Still, most of the year's crypto deal-making surge took place prior to a market rout in October upended bitcoin (BTC-USD) and other digital asset prices, which have pared losses recently, but remain well below highs.
